action.reasoning: Observation: Recent iterations have all failed with 0% success in local tests due to bugs in the heuristic planner (likely incorrect node indexing, path reconstruction, or action selection). The best known score is 0.5502 from iteration 13 using greedy-utility + BFS to global max. Analysis: I must revert to a clean implementation of that proven algorithm, ensure it works on local tests, then submit eval to regain the baseline. With only 7 iterations left, I will write a robust predict.py with careful coordinate rounding, BFS, tabu, and epsilon-greedy. I'll test it on 15 training maps to verify >50% success before submitting. Decision: Write new predict.py with a simple, well-tested HeuristicPlanner, test locally, then submit evaluation. If success, use remaining iterations to refine path efficiency (e.g., distance-aware BFS, smarter frontiers).
